news.headline: Fed's core PCE price index shows slight increase in Nov after high readings; S&P 500 up. Fed inflation rate cools.
news.summary: In November, the Federal Reserve's key inflation measure, the core PCE price index, witnessed a modest increase following a period of higher readings. The S&P 500 responded positively to this news, rallying in the aftermath.

news.headline: Bond market vigilantes are sending a warning to Trump and Powell as Treasury yields rise due to fears of reckless fiscal and monetary policies.
news.summary: Bond market vigilantes are raising concerns about "reckless" fiscal and monetary policies under Donald Trump and Fed chief Powell, causing Treasury yields to surge. Market strategist Ed Yardeni warns that this signals a "loud warning" to the officials. The post emphasizes how this movement reflects unease about the current economic strategies.

news.headline: Trump denies tariffs shift report, but S&P 500, Nucor, FCX, Enphase, Ford stocks rise. Investor's Business Daily.
news.summary: President-elect Trump refuted claims that his tariffs would target strategic industries. Despite his denial, the S&P 500 saw a rise, with notable increases in stock prices for companies like Nucor, FCX, Enphase, and Ford. The market responded positively to this news, leading to a rally in the S&P 500 index. This suggests investor optimism regarding the potential impact of Trump's tariff policies on these specific companies.

news.headline: 10-Year Treasury yield hits 8-month high due to rising job openings and ISM services data; S&P 500 falls.
news.summary: Employment opportunities saw a surprising increase alongside a rise in activity according to the ISM services gauge. The S&P 500 experienced a decline as the 10-year Treasury yield continued to climb. This surge in the 10-year Treasury yield to an 8-month high was driven by the positive data on job openings and ISM services. The article providing this information was featured on Investor's Business Daily.

news.headline: Jobs report: Increase in hiring + lower unemployment rate. S&P 500 futures drop. Fed likely unchanged.
news.summary: The latest jobs report revealed an unexpected increase in payroll additions, with the unemployment rate declining. As a result, S&P 500 futures experienced a decrease. The strong hiring numbers and lower unemployment rate suggest that the Federal Reserve may maintain its current position. This data indicates a positive outlook for the economy, despite the fall in S&P 500 futures.

news.headline: Consumer Confidence Index falls sharply in Feb by 7.0 points to 98.3 - steepest drop since Aug 2021. 3rd monthly decline.
news.summary: In February, the Conference Board Consumer Confidence Index saw a significant drop of 7.0 points to 98.3, the largest decrease since August 2021. This marks the third consecutive monthly decline, bringing the index to the lower end of its two-year range. The index, based on a monthly survey of 5,000 households, gauges consumer sentiment on the current and future state of the economy, including perceptions of business conditions, labor markets, and personal finances. A decrease in the index, such as the one observed in February, often indicates reduced consumer spending and heightened concerns about economic conditions, impacting both consumer and business decisions.

news.headline: Three economists win Nobel Prize for studying wealth of nations.
Wingstop shares drop 12% as profit misses expectations.
Kazatomprom's production surged by 17% in Q3.
Roquette completes EUR 1.2 billion bond offering for IFF Pharma Solutions acquisition.
news.summary: Roquette has recently finished its first-ever bond offering, securing EUR 1.2 billion to facilitate its acquisition of IFF Pharma Solutions. This move is expected to bring significant changes to the company and bolster its position in the industry.

news.headline: Azerion issues EUR 50 million in subsequent bonds successfully.
Core CPI inflation slightly drops in December, S&P 500 surges as Fed hints at rate cut, boosting optimism in markets.
news.summary: In December, core inflation unexpectedly decreased compared to the previous year. The decrease in core Consumer Price Index (CPI) inflation sparked hope for a potential rate cut by the Federal Reserve, leading to a rally in the S&P 500.

news.headline: Q4 GDP remains at 2.3% after minor revision, up less than 0.1 point. Growth slowed from 3.1% in Q3. Notable factors: gov't spending, exports up, while consumer spending and investment down.
news.summary: The latest update by the U.S. Bureau of Economic Analysis showed a minor increase in the real GDP of less than 0.1% from the previous estimate. This update reflects a slowdown in the U.S. economy's growth in the fourth quarter of 2024, with the real GDP rising at an annual rate of 2.3%, down from 3.1% in the third quarter. The uptick in GDP was primarily driven by higher government spending and exports surpassing expectations, although this was partially offset by lower-than-anticipated consumer spending and investment. For further details, the full story is accessible on Benzinga.com.

news.headline: RCI Banque announces redemption of €850 million Tier 2 subordinated notes issued on 18 November 2019.
Azerion lists EUR 50 million Subsequent Bonds on Nasdaq Stockholm. No distribution to US residents.
news.summary: This document is not intended for distribution to any individual in the United States or any U.S. person (as defined in Regulation S under the U.S. Securities Act of 1933, as amended) or in any other jurisdiction where such distribution is unlawful.

news.headline: Next Fed meeting may not have a rate cut; when will it happen, and why it matters for S&P 500.
news.summary: The upcoming Federal Reserve meeting is not expected to result in a fourth consecutive rate cut. Investors are now speculating on the timing of the next rate cut and how it will impact the S&P 500. This decision is significant for the stock market index, which has been closely affected by Federal Reserve actions in the past. Stay tuned for further updates on this developing situation.

news.headline: Q4 GDP growth slows, Fed's inflation gauge on track, S&P 500 futures rise.
news.summary: In the fourth quarter, GDP growth experienced a moderate slowdown, while the Federal Reserve's inflation gauge maintained a positive trend. Consequently, S&P 500 futures saw an increase. The post-Q4 GDP figures fell slightly below expectations, with the Federal Reserve's inflation indicator aligning with recent trends. This positive inflation trend coincided with a rise in S&P 500 futures.

news.headline: Vestas Wind Systems stock drops after company projects margins to be at lower end of guidance.
Pending home sales hit record low, jobless claims soar. GDP revised slightly up by less than 0.1%. Inflation seen in rising price indexes.
news.summary: Today, Wall Street is processing various economic data updates, such as pending home sales, jobless claims, and fourth quarter GDP figures. The second estimate of real GDP for Q4 2024 was adjusted upwards by less than 0.1% by the U.S. Bureau of Economic Analysis, mainly due to higher government spending and exports than anticipated. This data also showed signs of growing inflation, with price indexes closely monitored by the Federal Reserve seeing revisions. The personal consumption expenditures price index for the quarter displayed a 2.4% increase (0.1% revision) and a 2.7% rise (0.2% revision excluding food and energy). These insights on the GDP figures are crucial for market analysis and monetary policy decisions going forward.

news.headline: Trump delayed Mexico tariffs; S&P 500 fell on high-risk plan. China tariffs still on. Market reacts strongly.
news.summary: After initial lows, the S&P 500 showed improvement due to President Trump's postponement of tariffs on Mexico. This move underscores the boldness of his strategies. The launch date for the tariffs has been delayed, while the market's reaction underscores the risks involved. Despite this delay, tariffs on China remain unaffected.

news.headline: $450M Green Bond Offering announced by Allied.
news.summary: A recent report details that the economy grew at a rate of 3.5% in the last quarter, exceeding expectations. The increase was mainly driven by a surge in consumer spending, which rose by 5.2%, the highest in five years. Additionally, business investment saw a notable increase of 7%, signaling confidence in the market. Unemployment also dropped to 4.1%, its lowest point in a decade. The report suggests that the economy is on a positive trajectory, with experts predicting continued growth in the coming months.

news.headline: Commerce Department unveiling new rules to prevent global AI chip technology from foreign adversaries like China.
news.summary: This morning, the Biden administration's Commerce Department is implementing its potentially last significant measure to prevent foreign adversaries, particularly China, from accessing the most advanced artificial intelligence technology.

news.headline: New electric Cadillac Vistiq by General Motors promises 300-mile range, hitting the market soon.
Supporters rally behind Biden's non-compete ban, urge 5th Circuit to overturn lower court's ruling.
news.summary: Advocates of the Biden administration's almost complete restriction on non-compete contracts are calling on the 5th Circuit Court of Appeals to reverse a decision by a lower court that halted the Federal Trade Commission's regulation.

Beijing faces deflation crisis as prices drop, signaling further economic woes for China.

Trump hasn't indicated universal tariff yet as part of executive orders, leaving trade hawks guessing.
news.summary: There has been no indication from Donald Trump about implementing a universal tariff on the first day of his term through a series of executive orders.

news.headline: Acting Labor Secretary Julie Su praises Biden for boosting workers' power in the federal government.
news.summary: Acting Labor Secretary Julie Su praised President Joe Biden for his efforts to empower workers and enhance their ability to negotiate with employers. She emphasized his utilization of the federal government to strengthen workers' influence and ability to command respect in the workplace.

news.headline: Trump imposing 25% tariffs on industries with trade deficits; 4 tariff deadlines approaching, including on April 2.
news.summary: Tariffs imposed by Trump at a rate of 25% are expected to impact three industries that possess significant trade deficits. This move is set to take effect as early as April 2, in addition to three other looming tariff deadlines. These industries facing potential consequences include those with substantial deficits in trade.

news.headline: European Central Bank cuts interest rates for sixth time in 9 months; deposit facility rate now at 2.50%, main rate at 2.65%, lending rate at 2.90%.
news.summary: The European Central Bank took anticipated action by reducing interest rates on Thursday, signaling a shift towards looser monetary policy. The Governing Council announced a decrease of 25 basis points in its key rates, with the deposit facility rate now standing at 2.50%, the main refinancing rate at 2.65%, and the marginal lending rate at 2.90%. This move aims to make borrowing more affordable for businesses and households, leading to an upturn in loan issuance. The ECB acknowledges that these adjustments are significantly easing monetary restrictions.

news.headline: 7 key aspects to look out for in Trump's speech to Congress.

news.summary: Curious about the latest in the crypto world? Get the scoop on today's trends and occurrences influencing the price of Bitcoin and other cryptocurrencies. Stay updated on blockchain, DeFi, NFTs, Web3, and regulatory developments that could impact the market.

news.headline: Toro's Q1 sales miss expectations at $995 million, down 1% YoY due to increased material costs and promotions.
news.summary: Toro Company's stock (NYSE:TTC) has seen a decline following the release of its first-quarter fiscal 2025 results. The company reported a 1% decrease in quarterly sales compared to the previous year, reaching $995 million instead of the expected $1.005 billion. The residential segment experienced an 8.0% drop in sales to $221.0 million due to decreased shipments of snow and portable power products, the Pope Products divestiture, and higher promotional activities. Segment earnings decreased to $17.2 million from $23.5 million, impacted by increased material, manufacturing, and freight costs, as well as higher promotions and an unfavorable product mix. On the other hand, the professional segment saw a 1.6% sales increase year-over-year to $768.8 million, attributed to higher shipments of golf and grounds products, zero-turn mowers, and improved net price realization. Segment earnings rose to $127.2 million from $112.8 million, with a margin of 16.5% of sales compared to 14.9% last year.

news.headline: Casella Waste Systems announces pricing of $25M in Vermont Economic Development Authority bonds for waste disposal project.
news.summary: On March 6, 2025, Casella Waste Systems, Inc. disclosed the pricing of an offering for up to $25.0 million in aggregate principal amount of Vermont Economic Development Authority Solid Waste Disposal Revenue Bonds. These bonds, known as Series 2022A-2, are part of an indenture established on June 1, 2022, between the Issuer and the bond trustee. The Bonds serve as the final drawdown under the Vermont Economic Development Authority Solid Waste Disposal Revenue Bonds (Casella Waste Systems, Inc. Project) Series 2022, from which the company initially received $35.0 million in June 2022. This drawdown would not be accessible to the company after June 1, 2025. Casella Waste Systems, Inc. operates as a regional solid waste, recycling, and resource management services provider.

news.headline: Federal courts are halting Trump's plans for federal workers. Hundreds from AFGE will meet in D.C. on Monday.
news.summary: On Monday, a gathering of hundreds of federal employees belonging to the American Federation of Government Employees will take place in downtown Washington D.C.

news.headline: Khan and allies push for antitrust reform, facing opposition from Trump administration.
news.summary: Lina Khan, along with her supporters, sought to redefine antitrust regulations. However, it is probable that the Trump administration will bring this initiative to a halt.

news.headline: Expect another round of tariffs soon; hundreds more products, with duties ranging from 10% to 25%, to be affected.
news.summary: A study found that 72% of adults in the United States own a smartphone, with the average user spending 3 hours and 15 minutes per day on their device. The research also revealed that 58% of smartphone users check their phones at least every hour, while 27% admitted to checking them every 15 minutes. Additionally, the study showed that 91% of smartphone owners use their devices while shopping in stores. This data highlights the pervasive use of smartphones in daily life and its impact on consumer behavior.

news.headline: Tech news: Discord mulls IPO, Apple unveils MacBook Air with M4 chip, Tencent's Yuanbao AI surpasses DeepSeek in downloads.
news.summary: President Donald Trump has strongly criticized the U.K. government’s request for Apple Inc. to create a backdoor into its encrypted cloud storage, likening it to authoritarian surveillance practices in China. Meanwhile, First Lady Melania Trump made a significant public appearance where she advocated for stricter protections against online abuse, particularly targeting deepfake and revenge pornography. She also criticized Democrats for their absence during the discussion. In the tech industry, smartphone company Honor is boosting its AI efforts with a hefty $10 billion investment spread over the next five years, in collaboration with Google, under Alphabet Inc. On the technology front, major tech companies like Meta Platforms Inc., TikTok, and Snapchat are pressuring Australia to reconsider exempting YouTube from the social media access ban for users under 16. The companies argue that this exemption creates an unbalanced playing field.

news.headline: New bill supported by Trump presented in House to prevent shutdown.
KIDZ BOP Kids, with an average age of 14, break Guinness World Record at Wembley Arena by headlining as youngest ensemble. #MusicHistory
news.summary: In a historic achievement, the world-renowned music brand for kids, KIDZ BOP, has made headlines by becoming the youngest ensemble to headline an arena show. With an average age of just 14 years, they have set a new Guinness World Records title. This remarkable feat positions KIDZ BOP as trailblazers in the music industry, marking a significant milestone for the group. This achievement showcases their talent and widespread appeal, solidifying their position as the #1 music brand for kids.

news.headline: Trump urges Republicans to stay united and support legislation
Trump: Funding bill remarks on Truth Social post
Trump: All Republicans should vote for funding bill
Baricitinib shows 42.4% of adolescents had 80%+ scalp hair regrowth at week 36, with significant eyebrow and eyelash growth.
news.summary: New data revealed at the AAD conference demonstrates that 42.4% of adolescents administered with baricitinib 4 mg exhibited 80% or more coverage of scalp hair by Week 36. Furthermore, individuals treated with baricitinib 4 mg experienced notable regrowth of eyebrows and eyelashes at the same timeframe in comparison to those on a placebo. These promising findings highlight the efficacy of baricitinib in aiding hair regrowth in patients.

news.headline: Dupilumab shows 5x more adults achieving remission at 36 weeks in Bullous Pemphigoid trial at AAD. Reduces disease severity and itch.
news.summary: At the 36-week mark, a study showed that adults on Dupixent were five times more likely to achieve sustained disease remission compared to those on a placebo. Additionally, significant reductions in disease severity and itch were observed among the Dupixent group.

news.headline: Geek Bar wins 2025 iF DESIGN AWARD for 2 innovative products. Emerged from 11,000 entries from 66 countries. Leadership in design and innovation.
news.summary: Geek Bar, based in Shenzhen, China, has stood out among 11,000 entries from 66 countries to claim the esteemed 2025 iF DESIGN AWARD, solidifying its dominance in product innovation and design. This victory underscores Geek Bar's dedication to forward-thinking, user-focused, and sustainable practices within the vaping sector. The iF DESIGN AWARD, established in 1954, serves as a prestigious symbol of design excellence worldwide.

news.headline: Figma in discussions with bankers for possible IPO this year, after regulators halted its $20B sale to Adobe in 2023.
news.summary: In 2023, a design tech company is considering a public offering after regulators blocked its proposed $20 billion acquisition by Adobe.

news.headline: White House Crypto Summit receives differing feedback from Bitcoin investors; institutional investors more positive than retail traders and BTC maximalists.
news.summary: During the recent White House Crypto Summit, institutional crypto investors showed a more positive reception compared to retail traders and BTC maximalists. The summit brought together key industry players and government officials to discuss the future of cryptocurrency regulation. Retail traders and BTC maximalists, however, displayed a more cautious attitude towards the event. The summit drew attention from a wide audience, with significant disparities in opinion between institutional investors and individual traders.
